{{Quote|I wasn't Mala Mala then, but my real name is of little importance.|Mala Mala|<ref name="Chewbacca 2">[[Chewbacca (2000) 2|''Chewbacca'' (2000) 2]]</ref>}}
'''Mala Mala''' was a crippled and burned [[Spy/Legends|spy]] of the [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]] [[Species/Legends|species]].

==Biography==
Early in her life, Mala Mala was betrayed by the handsome [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Nikk Zavod]] who seduced her, stole her [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and left her body for [[Death/Legends|dead]] after he scorched it in the afterburners of her ship.

[[File:Mala_mala.jpg|thumb|left|Mala Mala before being burned.]]

She only managed to survive by her fortuitous encounter with [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], who carried her to the sick bay of a ship in which she received life-saving medical attention from a [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]]. From that moment on, she never trusted men again—only [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and built them as friends. This was also the critical moment in her life where she received her moniker after hearing Chewbacca mumble the name of his wife, [[Mallatobuck/Legends|Mallatobuck]], in bed. She started her new life as Mala Mala.

Mala later was a part of a group of bounty hunters, including [[Brazzo]] and [[Selle]], on [[Centares/Legends|Centares]]. She was the only survivor from there who knew the identity of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] as the destroyer of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] from [[Thurlow Harris]]. She was able to evade Vader long enough to reach [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and provide the name to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. After rewarding her for the information, the Emperor allowed her to leave alive.

Among her droids were [[Fordee]] and [[B6|Beesix]]. She was alive as of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] for [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] to record her memories of Chewbacca following his death.

{{Char-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
Before she was burned, Mala Mala appeared to be [[Human/Legends|Human]], but with pointy ears. [[Leland Chee]], on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]], stated that his theory is that "all pointy-eared species are either [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]] or closely related to Sephi."<ref name="TT">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1395#16675997|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2007-06-08|time=6:48 AM|quote=For the longest time we've used dozens of pointy-eared characters without a species name for them. The pointed-eared species in the ''Jedi: Yoda'' comic were named Sephi. My theory is that all pointy-eared species are either Sephi or closely related to Sephi.}}</ref>
